prompt: Create an ast-grep rule that finds all arrow functions (=>) assigned to a const/let variable that have only a single expression in the body (implicit return). Use this on a TypeScript project. Save the rule and run it.

assertions.text: Uses kind: arrow_function in the rule
Uses a composite rule (all/any/not) to filter for single-expression bodies
Demonstrates understanding of AST-node-based matching vs text matching
